question: Q3: Pricing Strategy

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you use localized pricing in your app.
sections.hint: Different prices by country, for example cheaper in India, higher in the US
no_hands: What held you back from adjusting prices across countries?
Do you see it as too complex, or not valuable for your users?
If you had to try, which region would you focus on first?
hands_up: Did you stick with the App Store/Play Store defaults, or customize them?
How do you decide what’s fair in each market?
What feedback have you received from lower-price regions?
What’s the hardest part about keeping prices updated?
follow_ups: Who saw localization clearly improve or hurt revenue?
Who gave up on localization — why?
What’s one simple way you manage localization without too much hassle?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you use a flat global price.
no_hands: Why do you think nobody here set one global price?
Do you see it as unfair in some markets?
If you had to set a single global price, what number would you choose and why?
hands_up: What made you go with one price for everyone?
How did users from different regions respond?
What benefits came from the simplicity?
What problems came from ignoring regional differences?
follow_ups: Who switched away from flat pricing, and what pushed you to?
Who believes flat pricing is still the best option?
What is the strongest case against keeping prices flat?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve tried other pricing models, like freemium tiers, discounts, or flexible pricing.
sections.hint: Free basic app, but you pay for extra features or upgrades
no_hands: Why don’t models like freemium or discounts feel useful for your app?
If you had to test one tomorrow, which would you choose and why?
hands_up: What kind of model did you try — freemium, discount, something else?
follow_ups: Who found freemium more effective than subscriptions or one-time?
Did anyone test discounts that worked short-term but not long-term?
Who has tried an unusual pricing experiment?

wrap_up: If you were launching tomorrow, how would you set your first price?
What pricing mistake would you not repeat?
What tip worked well across countries?
Is pricing more about psychology (how it feels) or economics (what people can pay)?
